topic: The Good Qualities of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW)

subUnits.title: 9.1 The Prophet's Character and Moral Excellence
notes: Introduction:
• The Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was sent as a mercy to all mankind
• His excellent character was the main factor that attracted people to Islam
• Allah praised his character in the Qur'an
Quranic Evidence of the Prophet's Character:
Surah Al-Qalam (68:4):
وَإِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍ
And indeed, you are of a great moral character
Surah Al-Ahzab (33:21):
لَّقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ
There has certainly been for you in the Messenger of Allah an excellent pattern
Key Qualities of the Prophet (SAW):
1. Truthfulness (As-Sidq):
• He was known as 'As-Sadiq' (The Truthful) even before Prophethood
• Never lied in speech or action
• His enemies acknowledged his truthfulness
• Example: When he warned Quraysh of punishment, they believed him because he never lied
2. Trustworthiness (Al-Amanah):
• Known as 'Al-Amin' (The Trustworthy)
• People entrusted him with their valuables
• Even during persecution, he returned trusts to their owners
• Example: Before Hijrah, he returned all trusts to Makkans
3. Mercy and Compassion (Ar-Rahmah):
• Showed mercy to all creation: humans, animals, environment
• Forgave enemies who persecuted him for years
• Wept for his Ummah and prayed for their guidance
Quranic Evidence:
Surah At-Tawbah (9:128):
لَقَدْ جَاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ عَزِيزٌ عَلَيْهِ مَا عَنِتُّمْ حَرِيصٌ عَلَيْكُم بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
There has certainly come to you a Messenger from among yourselves. Grievous to him is what you suffer; he is concerned over you and to the believers is kind and merciful
4. Patience and Perseverance (As-Sabr):
• Endured persecution, mockery, and boycott with patience
• Never retaliated with anger or vengeance
• Prayed for guidance of his persecutors
• Example: At Ta'if, when stoned, he prayed for the people's guidance
5. Humility (At-Tawadu):
• Despite being the leader, he lived simply
• Ate with servants, mended his own clothes
• Greeted everyone, including children
• Never boasted or showed arrogance
6. Justice and Fairness (Al-Adl):
• Treated all people equally regardless of status
• Judged fairly even against his own relatives
• Protected rights of weak and oppressed
Hadith on Justice:
The Prophet (SAW) said:
اعْدِلُوا هُوَ أَقْرَبُ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ
Be just, for that is nearer to righteousness
7. Generosity (Al-Karam):
• Never refused anyone who asked for help
• Gave away everything he had
• Described as 'more generous than the blowing wind'
• Example: Gave away a whole herd of goats to a new Muslim
8. Courage and Bravery:
• Stood firm in battles despite being outnumbered
• Never fled from danger when truth was at stake
• Protected his Companions at personal risk
9. Wisdom and Knowledge (Al-Hikmah):
• Gave wise counsel in all matters
• Resolved disputes with fairness and insight
• Taught with methods suited to people's understanding
10. Purity and Cleanliness:
• Always maintained personal hygiene
• Loved pleasant scents
• Encouraged cleanliness as part of faith
Hadith on Cleanliness:
The Prophet (SAW) said:
الطُّهُورُ شَطْرُ الْإِيمَانِ
Purification is half of faith
Impact of These Qualities:
• People embraced Islam after witnessing his character
• Even enemies became Muslims after experiencing his mercy
• His character remains a model for all Muslims
• Following his example leads to success in this life and the Hereafter
Lessons for Muslims Today:
• Emulate the Prophet's character in daily life
• Truthfulness and trustworthiness build strong communities
• Mercy and forgiveness heal divisions
• Patience in hardship brings reward from Allah
• Humility elevates a person in the sight of Allah
Conclusion:
• The Prophet's character was the living Qur'an
• His life is the perfect example for humanity
• Following his Sunnah is the path to Allah's pleasure
• His qualities transformed Arabia and the world

quiz.q: The verse 'And indeed, you are of a great moral character' is in Surah:
quiz.explanation: This verse praising the Prophet's character is in Surah Al-Qalam (68:4).

quiz.q: The title 'Al-Amin' given to the Prophet means:
quiz.explanation: Al-Amin means 'The Trustworthy' - a title given by Makkans due to his honesty and reliability.

quiz.q: The Prophet's quality of mercy is evidenced by:
opts: Forgiving enemies at the Conquest of Makkah
quiz.explanation: At the Conquest of Makkah, the Prophet forgave those who persecuted him for 20 years, demonstrating supreme mercy.

quiz.q: The verse describing the Prophet as 'kind and merciful to believers' is in Surah:
quiz.explanation: Surah At-Tawbah (9:128) describes the Prophet's concern and mercy towards the believers.

quiz.q: The Prophet's patience was demonstrated when:
opts: He prayed for the guidance of those who stoned him at Ta'if
quiz.explanation: After being stoned and rejected at Ta'if, the Prophet prayed for the people's guidance instead of cursing them.

quiz.q: The Hadith 'Purification is half of faith' emphasizes the Prophet's emphasis on:
quiz.explanation: This Hadith shows the Prophet's emphasis on physical and spiritual cleanliness as part of faith.

quiz.q: The Prophet's generosity was such that he:
opts: Never refused anyone who asked for help
quiz.explanation: The Prophet was described as more generous than the blowing wind, never refusing anyone in need.

quiz.q: The Prophet's justice was shown by:
opts: Judging fairly even against his own relatives
quiz.explanation: The Prophet established justice for all, even ruling against his own family when they were wrong.

quiz.q: A lesson from the Prophet's character for Muslims today is:
opts: Truthfulness and mercy build strong communities
quiz.explanation: The Prophet's example teaches that truthfulness, mercy, and good character are essential for personal and community success.

quiz.q: The Prophet's character is described in the Qur'an as:
quiz.explanation: Allah Himself testified to the Prophet's great moral character in Surah Al-Qalam (68:4).

subUnits.title: 9.2 How the Prophet's Qualities Attracted People to Islam
notes: Introduction:
• People embraced Islam not just by hearing the message, but by witnessing the Messenger
• The Prophet's character was the practical demonstration of Qur'anic teachings
• His life was the living proof of Islam's truth
Ways the Prophet's Character Attracted People:
1. Through Truthfulness and Honesty:
• Even before Prophethood, people trusted him completely
• When he began preaching, people knew he wouldn't lie about Allah
• Example: Abu Bakr immediately believed because he knew Muhammad never lied
Hadith:
The Prophet (SAW) said:
عَلَيْكُمْ بِالصِّدْقِ فَإِنَّ الصِّدْقَ يَهْدِي إِلَى الْبِرِّ
Adhere to truthfulness, for truthfulness leads to righteousness
2. Through Mercy and Compassion:
• People saw his care for the weak, poor, and oppressed
• His mercy extended to enemies, animals, and the environment
• Example: He wept when a companion's child died, showing human compassion
Quranic Evidence:
Surah Al-Imran (3:159):
فَبِمَا رَحْمَةٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ لِنتَ لَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ كُنتَ فَظًّا غَلِيظَ الْقَلْبِ لَانفَضُّوا مِنْ حَوْلِكَ
So by mercy from Allah, you were lenient with them. And if you had been rude [in speech] and harsh in heart, they would have disbanded from about you
3. Through Fairness and Justice:
• People of all backgrounds felt protected under his leadership
• Jews, Christians, and pagans received fair treatment
• Example: The Constitution of Madinah guaranteed rights for all communities
4. Through Humility and Simplicity:
• Despite being a leader, he lived like the poor
• Ate simple food, wore simple clothes, slept on a mat
• People saw that his message wasn't for personal gain
Hadith:
The Prophet (SAW) said:
مَا مَلَأَ آدَمِيٌّ وِعَاءً شَرًّا مِنْ بَطْنِهِ
The son of Adam does not fill any vessel worse than his stomach
5. Through Courage and Conviction:
• He stood firm despite persecution, boycott, and threats
• People saw his unwavering commitment to truth
• Example: At the offer of wealth and power to stop preaching, he refused
6. Through Wisdom in Da'wah:
• He spoke to people according to their understanding
• Used gentle persuasion, not force
• Example: When a Bedouin urinated in the mosque, he taught gently instead of punishing
7. Through Personal Example:
• He practiced what he preached
• His life was consistent with his message
• People saw Islam as a complete way of life, not just words
Stories of Conversion Through Character:
1. Conversion of Umar ibn Al-Khattab:
• Initially opposed Islam fiercely
• Was moved by the courage of Muslims and the beauty of Qur'anic recitation
• Witnessed the Prophet's character and embraced Islam
2. Conversion of Khalid ibn Al-Walid:
• Fought against Muslims at Uhud
• Was impressed by Muslim unity and the Prophet's leadership
• Embraced Islam and became 'The Sword of Allah'
3. Conversion of the People of Makkah:
• After 20 years of persecution, the Prophet forgave them at Conquest
• His mercy moved many to accept Islam willingly
4. Conversion of Delegations:
• Delegations from various tribes came to Madinah
• Witnessed the Prophet's justice, wisdom, and mercy
• Returned to their people and invited them to Islam
Quranic Evidence of Character's Impact:
Surah Ali 'Imran (3:110):
كُنتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ تَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَتَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ
You are the best nation produced for mankind. You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong
This excellence comes from following the Prophet's example
Lessons for Da'wah Today:
1. Character is the Best Advertisement for Islam:
• People are attracted by good character more than arguments
• Muslims should embody Islamic values in daily life
2. Mercy Opens Hearts:
• Harshness repels; mercy attracts
• Following the Prophet's mercy in dealing with others
3. Consistency Builds Trust:
• Practicing what we preach makes our message credible
• Hypocrisy damages the image of Islam
4. Wisdom in Approach:
• Speak to people according to their level of understanding
• Use gentle persuasion, not confrontation
5. Patience in Da'wah:
• Change takes time; the Prophet's mission took 23 years
• Persevere with good character despite challenges
Application for Nigerian Muslims:
• In a diverse society, good character builds bridges between communities
• Truthfulness in business and dealings attracts people to Islam
• Mercy and forgiveness heal ethnic and religious tensions
• Justice and fairness combat corruption and build trust
• Humility counters arrogance and pride in society
Conclusion:
• The Prophet's character was the most powerful tool of da'wah
• His life remains the perfect model for inviting people to Islam
• Muslims today must embody these qualities to effectively convey the message
• Following the Prophet's example is the key to personal and communal success
Final Hadith:
The Prophet (SAW) said:
إِنَّمَا بُعِثْتُ لِأُتَمِّمَ مَكَارِمَ الْأَخْلَاقِ
I was only sent to perfect good character
This shows that character is the essence of the Islamic message
diagramDescriptions: Flowchart: Prophet's qualities → Witnessed by people → Trust built → Acceptance of Islam
Stories of conversion: Umar, Khalid, Makkans - showing character's impact
Da'wah methodology diagram: Truthfulness, Mercy, Wisdom, Patience → Effective invitation
Nigerian context illustration: Applying Prophet's character in diverse society
Hadith compilation: Key sayings on character and their practical applications

quiz.q: The verse 'If you had been rude and harsh in heart, they would have disbanded from about you' emphasizes:
opts: The importance of mercy in da'wah
quiz.explanation: Surah Al-Imran (3:159) teaches that mercy and gentleness are essential for keeping people close and attracting them to Islam.

quiz.q: Abu Bakr immediately believed the Prophet because:
quiz.explanation: Abu Bakr's immediate acceptance was based on his knowledge of the Prophet's lifelong truthfulness.

quiz.q: The Prophet's response to the Bedouin who urinated in the mosque was to:
quiz.explanation: The Prophet demonstrated wisdom by teaching gently rather than punishing, which attracted people to Islam.

quiz.q: The Hadith 'I was only sent to perfect good character' shows that:
opts: Character is the essence of the Islamic message
quiz.explanation: This Hadith emphasizes that perfecting good character is the core purpose of the Prophet's mission.

quiz.q: Umar ibn Al-Khattab embraced Islam after witnessing:
opts: The courage of Muslims and the Prophet's character
quiz.explanation: Umar was moved by the Muslims' courage and the Prophet's character, leading to his conversion.

quiz.q: At the Conquest of Makkah, the Prophet attracted people by:
opts: Granting general amnesty and showing mercy
quiz.explanation: The Prophet's mercy in forgiving his enemies at Makkah's conquest moved many to accept Islam willingly.

quiz.q: A lesson for da'wah today from the Prophet's character is:
opts: Good character attracts people more than arguments
quiz.explanation: The Prophet's example shows that embodying Islamic values is more effective in inviting people than mere argumentation.

quiz.q: The Constitution of Madinah demonstrated the Prophet's:
opts: Justice and fairness to all communities
quiz.explanation: The Constitution guaranteed rights for Muslims, Jews, and other communities, showing the Prophet's commitment to justice.

quiz.q: For Nigerian Muslims, applying the Prophet's character means:
opts: Building bridges through truthfulness, mercy, and justice
quiz.explanation: In Nigeria's diverse society, embodying the Prophet's character helps build trust and attract people to Islam.

quiz.q: The Prophet's consistency between words and actions was important because it:
opts: Made his message credible and trustworthy
quiz.explanation: The Prophet's practice of what he preached made Islam credible and attracted people to follow his example.
